LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED FOR FAILURE TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF. DEFENDANT FAILS TO PRESENT EVIDENCE THAT PLAINTIFF DOES NOT POSSESS AND CANNOT REASONABLY OBTAIN EVIDENCE TO PROVE HER CASE. SEE WEBER V. CRANE (2006) 143 CAL.APP.4TH 1433. FUTHERMORE, TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED REGARDING THE EXISTENCE OF A DANGEROUS CONDITION. (PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION; DEFENDANT'S EXHIBIT B). PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. ORDER SIGNED RE: DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO DECLARATION OF KEIKO TSUYUKI AND EXHIBITS TO THE CHOY DECLARATION. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE CSR #5797
MINI-MINUTES FOR OCT-21-2010 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-21-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Appearances: Randall P. Choy of Hedani Choy Spalding & Salvagione LLP (778.0800) for plaintiff and responding party; Cyrus A. Tabari of Sheuerman Martini & Tabari (408.288.9700) for defendant and moving party. Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied, failure to shift burden of proof. Defendant fails to present evidence that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence to prove her case. See Weber v. Crane (2006) 143 Cal.App.4th 1433. Futhermore, triable issue of fact raised regarding the existence of a dangerous condition. (plaintiff's declaration; defendant's exhibit B). Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Order signed re: defendant's objections to declaration of Keiko Tsuyuki and exhibits to the Choy declaration. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR #5797
